steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The blank sits between “wildlife” and the verb “is,” so it must form the subject with “wildlife.” The blank contains a title (“biologist”) followed by a proper name (“Dr. Purnima Devi Barman”), where the name specifies which biologist is being discussed.

steps.title: Apply the restrictive appositive rule
steps.content:
A name that directly identifies the noun needs no punctuation—here, “Dr.

Purnima Devi Barman” identifies the biologist, so the title and name form a single unit.

Commas (A, B) would incorrectly treat the name as parenthetical information, and a colon (C) isn't used for appositives.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
